title: The implanter
intro: This is a real machine now: an aperture that absorbs anything off-axis, an accelerating column that pumps energy into whatever flies through, and a wafer strip waiting at the end. Dopants must arrive in an energy window — too slow and they stop at the surface, too fast and they bury too deep. Pull harder or softer to set the launch speed.
explain: You just operated an ion implanter. Real ones fire dopant ions at keV to MeV energies; the implant RANGE — how deep the ion stops — is set by the arrival energy you just tuned, which is why the Chapter 6 implant step has an energy knob. The aperture is real too (beam collimation), and tilting the beam a few degrees to avoid ion channeling is standard practice.
